summary: The goal of the Associated Graduate Students in Geography is to provide leadership, organization, and represent the interest of the geography graduate student community.

summary: University Theatre is here to provide an opportunity for UO students to participate in dramatic arts productions as well as for UO students and community members to see live theatrical performances of high caliber at affordable prices.

name: Coalition for Indigenous Rights and Environmental Justice
summary: University of Oregon community members working collaboratively with Indigenous communities in Bolivia to support indigenous rights and environmental justice.

summary: GAS is a student organization to discuss global issues, UN policy, social justice, and career development. We host rich discussions and invite guest speakers to engage in international issues and provide social networking opportunities!

summary: Bioethics Society is for students interested in the impact of science on humanity and our environment. We strive to provoke conversation and learning about the ethical issues of the life sciences through discussions, guest speakers, and volunteering!

name: Student Advocacy Program
summary: The ASUO Student Advocacy Program is a service funded by the ASUO Student Government and operated by Student Government Engagement and Success.

name: Federalist Society
summary: FedSoc's chief goal is to foster balanced and open debate about the fundamental principles of individual freedom and limited government. Our members hold diverse and often conflicting views on a broad range of issues.

summary: Taking Up Space is an open invitation to all creatives who want to connect and collaborate with other people of color. We prepare members for the industry ahead through passion projects and career development while also supporting a safe space for BIPOC.

name: Amnesty International- University of Oregon Chapter
summary: We meet every other Thursday from 6-7 p.m. beginning Week 2 in Condon 201 for Spring Term. Check out our Instagram @aisoregon for additional meeting information.

summary:

restoration to sustain biodiversity, improve resilience in a changing climate, and re-establish an

name: Neurodiversity Alliance at UO
summary: A space where neurodivergent students can connect and work together to mentor each other and brainstorm ideas to be successful in our academic and career goals, advocate for neurodivergent students at UO, and connect with resources.

name: National Association of Black Journalists
summary: Collective of BIPOC students who produce content and collaborate on portfolio work centering around social justice, entertainment and culture. We are a safe space for multimedia projects that reflect our student body's diverse upbringings.

name: Artificial Intelligence Student Association
summary: We welcome students from all backgrounds to join AISA, as we believe that interdisciplinary collaboration is critical to understanding the full potential of AI. If you are interested in learning more about AI and its impact on your field, we welcome you.

name: Labor and Employment Law Association (fka Labor and Employment Relations Association)
summary: LELA's mission to include many perspectives of labor and employment relations, contributes to the ASUO mission by bringing in differing viewpoints.

name: University of Oregon Media Studies Club
summary: Media Studies Club is a group for people who are excited about media.

name: The University of Oregon Data Science Club
summary: Passionate about data science? Join UODSCI! We're a community of passionate students advancing data science through projects, competitions, and educational opportunities, while promoting ethics and social good.

name: Alliance for Diversity in Science and Engineering
summary: Our goals are directed towards developing a formal professional and social network for science students at the UO and to provide a platform for underrepresented minorities in STEM (e.g. people of color, women, LGBTQIA+, those with disabilities)

name: Folklore Society at UO
summary: The UO Folklore Society supports folklore and ethnomusicology students, faculty and friends in their personal and professional endeavors. We seek to elevate diverse cultural research, practices, performances and traditional knowledge.

name: Ducks on the Air Amateur Radio Club
summary: Amateur radio club that promotes, advocates for, and fosters international and domestic communications, emergency communications, and community events. All majors are encouraged to join! (You don't need to have your license yet!)

name: Women In Economics Club
summary: A community to empower and encourage participation of women interested in Economics

name: Rural Student Coalition (RSC)
summary: The Rural Student Coalition seeks to ensure the academic, mental, and physical well-being of students from rural backgrounds as well as establishing community and accentuating the importance of rural communities on the University of Oregon campus.

name: Bookstagram: Behind the Scenes
summary: BBTS is a nontraditional book club that will discuss books and bookish topics that have become popular in social media. We'll talk about favorite genres, host bookish game nights, spread positivity for reading and the reader community, and so much more!

name: Journalism and Communication Graduate Student Association
summary: The Journalism and Communication Graduate Student Association (JCGSA) is a dedicated body committed to fostering a collaborative and supportive community for graduate students pursuing studies in journalism and communication.

name: Affinity Organization Coalition
summary: The purpose of the Affinity Organization Coalition is strategic advocacy on behalf of the University of Oregon School of Law’s minority community and to also provide a forum for those affected in this community.

name: Neuroscience Club
summary: The Neuroscience Club ignites curiosity and fosters understanding through captivating talks,interactive workshops, and thought-provoking discussions, unraveling the complexities of the human brain.To expand students passion for the field of neuroscience.

name: Portland Student Council at UO
summary: We aim to empower all students by fostering social enrichment and professional development, enhancing campus life through leadership and inclusive events.

name: Narcare UO
summary: Addressing the opioid crisis throughout the community by focusing on naloxone distribution, harm reduction, and empowerment through education.

name: The Rising Project
summary: he Rising Project is a student-run creative studio dedicated to merging creativity with science communication

name: Black Student Association
summary:
To cultivate a culturally rich community where students of color feel welcomed to
express themselves freely and creatively. Offer programming that forms connections,
engagement, and provides education on best practices that'll fuel success.

name: UO Sociology Club
summary: The University of Oregon Sociology Club works independently and in conjunction with the University of Oregon Sociology Department to foster a community among students surrounding the pursuit of Sociology through academic, professional, and social events.

name: National Organization of Minority Architecture Students UO
summary: The National Organization of Minority Architecture Students UO Chapter is a part of a national network of students and professionals in the architectural industry advancing diversity and equity

name: Game Development Club UO
summary: The Game Development Club provides opportunities for education and real game development experience for students, by hosting game jams, which are events we hold to allow teams to collaborate and create video games.

name: Freedom by Design at the UO
summary:
Freedom by Design™ is the AIAS community service program, this design-build based club uses the talents of College of Design students to radically impact the lives of people in their community.

Meetings every Tuesday at 6:15pm in LA 278

name: Perfect Pair
summary: Perfect Pair is a national non-profit organization that seeks to pair up older adults and college students to form meaningful connections.

name: Students Impacted by Chronic Conditions
summary: SICC is a student organization that provides peer support, resources, outreach and information for students managing chronic health conditions.

name: University of Oregon South Asian Law Student Association
summary: UOSALSA is an organization for South Asian students to get support to excel academically and professionally. This organization works closely with the South Asian Bar Association of Oregon, primarily to build a strong and well-connected community.

name: Helping Hands: Student-led Support For The Unhoused Community
summary: Helping Hands is a student-led group at UO supporting people experiencing homelessness in Eugene through care packages, outreach, and advocacy, while raising awareness of the systems behind housing insecurity.

name: OASIS
summary: Multi-disciplinary club that supports the new Environmental Design major by offering project work, community partnership and networking opportunities.

name: Pre-Eye Health Club
summary: This club connects students pursuing careers in Optometry, Ophthalmology, PA, and any other eye-health-related fields. We are both an educational and community-building resource that is open to anyone interested.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi
summary: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Gender Inclusive Professional Fraternity exists nationally to foster an inclusive brotherhood for undergraduate students interested in law and government.

name: Women's Accounting and Finance Club
summary: Please update the description of your organization

name: Accessible Education Center
summary: The AEC is dedicated to facilitating and supporting accessible education through access and full inclusion of students with disabilities into the university environment.

name: Women in Politics at UO
summary: The Women in Politics club is an academic club that provides a safe and open space for students to be educated on, openly research, and have conversations about current political events in a non partisan environment.

name: Women In Media
summary: WIM is a space for those who want to be seen, heard, and impossible to ignore, achieved through community, workshops, mentorship, and speaker events that build visibility, confidence, and professional readiness

name: Software in Business Club
summary: Our mission is to provide a community for students interested in software development, programming, and technology, while connecting members with networking, internships, and career opportunities in the technology field.

name: Citizens' Climate Lobby
summary: We are a University chapter of the larger nonprofit, nonpartisan, grassroots advocacy climate change organization focused on national policies to address the national and global climate crisis.

name: English Graduate Organization
summary: The English Graduate Organization (EGO) is a student-led body that serves to foster community, advocacy, and professional development among graduate students in the Department of English.

name: Screen Consciousness Club Oregon
summary: Helping people reduce and realize unconscious screen usage within daily life.

name: Brazilian Student Association at the University of Oregon
summary: BRASA UO is a student-led cultural organization that connects Brazilian and Brazilian-American students and promotes Brazilian culture through community events, language exchange, and educational programming.

name: Latinos in Business
summary: A business organization that empowers and inspires the Latinx community through professional development, leadership opportunities, and weekly workshops featuring Latinx speakers and panels, all grounded in cultural connection and community building.
